summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function: a nightly Timer-triggered Python 3.11 function that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI, validates the roster response, and persists structured JSON to a dedicated Azure Blob Storage account using Managed Identity for all service authentication.
assumptions: TRAPI supports Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens; if not, Key Vault will store the credential and a Key Vault reference will be used in App Settings.
TRAPI endpoint URL and API version will be confirmed by rciapala before APP-02 begins.
Player scope is limited to the 25-man active roster (24–28 range covers roster fluctuations); coaching staff and front-office are excluded unless re-scoped.
TRAPI is accessible over the public internet; no VNet integration required for initial deployment.
Consumption Plan is acceptable; cold-start risk at 2 AM UTC is acceptable pending SLA confirmation.
GitHub Actions OIDC federation is available for passwordless deployment to Azure.
Staging deployment slot is available on the Consumption Plan (slots require Standard or higher; if not available, a separate staging Function App will be used).
Existing Bicep infra modules under /infra will be extended rather than replaced.
OpenTelemetry SDK for Python is used for structured logging and custom metrics to Application Insights.
The prompt template is static and version-controlled; no dynamic prompt generation is required.
open_questions: TRAPI auth mechanism: Does TRAPI support Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens? What is the required scope? (Owner: rciapala)
TRAPI base URL and API version: Required before APP-02 implementation begins.
Player scope: Active 25-man roster only, or include coaching staff, injured list, and front-office? Affects prompt and validation thresholds.
Networking: Is TRAPI reachable over public internet or does it require VNet integration and private endpoints?
Hosting plan SLA: Is a strict 2 AM execution SLA required? If yes, Premium Plan with always-ready instances must replace Consumption Plan.
Does the Consumption Plan in the target subscription support deployment slots? If not, a separate staging Function App is required for slot-swap CI/CD.

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Provision all cloud infrastructure, establish identity and RBAC assignments, configure secrets management, and set up the CI/CD pipeline skeleton so the team can deploy and iterate safely.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint URL and auth scope confirmed by rciapala
Azure subscription and resource group available
GitHub repository access confirmed for Actions OIDC setup
Player scope decision made (active roster only vs. broader)
exit_criteria: All Bicep modules deploy successfully to staging resource group
Managed Identity role assignments verified (Storage Blob Data Contributor, Key Vault Secrets User)
GitHub Actions pipeline deploys a hello-world function to staging slot without errors
Key Vault created and TRAPI credential placeholder secret stored
Application Insights workspace provisioned and connected to Function App
PRJ-01 task plan review approved

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the core function logic: TRAPI client with retry/timeout, prompt template loading, response schema validation, blob writer with conditional PUT, and the Timer Trigger wiring.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint and auth scope confirmed
Prompt template content finalized and committed to prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt
exit_criteria: GetAndStoreYankeesRoster function executes end-to-end locally against TRAPI (or a mock)
Validated roster blob written to yankees-roster/{date}.json in dedicated storage account
Failed responses written to yankees-roster/failed/{date}.json
Unit tests pass for TRAPI client, schema validator, and blob writer
Timer Trigger fires on schedule 0 0 2 * * * with useMonitor: true confirmed in host.json

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability controls, security hardening, observability instrumentation, alerting rules, and integration/regression tests to make the solution production-grade.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function deploys successfully to staging environment
exit_criteria: Exponential-backoff retry (3 retries, 45s timeout) verified via unit and integration tests
Conditional PUT idempotency verified (same-day retrigger does not overwrite blob)
All three Application Insights alert rules active and tested (failure, duration, data quality)
Structured log events emitted for all key lifecycle events
player_count_returned custom metric visible in Application Insights
Security scan passes (no hardcoded secrets, no public blob access)
Integration test asserts Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson present in output blob

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Complete staging end-to-end validation, execute production slot swap via GitHub Actions, activate monitoring, and finalize documentation.
entry_criteria: All unit and integration tests passing in CI
exit_criteria: End-to-end staging execution verified at 2 AM UTC (or manual trigger)
Production slot swap completed via GitHub Actions without rollback
All alert rules active in production Application Insights workspace
README documents local dev setup, TRAPI auth instructions, and blob naming convention
All success criteria from spec.md checked off

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Stakeholder review and approval of this engineering task plan before implementation begins. Confirm open questions are resolved or have owners assigned, wave sequencing is agreed, and estimates are accepted.
acceptance_criteria: All open questions have a named owner and target resolution date
Wave sequencing and estimates reviewed by rciapala and platform engineer
Plan committed or linked from repository
TRAPI endpoint/auth and player scope decisions unblocked or tracked as risks
risks: TRAPI auth and endpoint unknowns may delay Foundation wave if not resolved before kickoff

tasks.title: Provision dedicated storage account and blob container via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend /infra Bicep modules to provision a dedicated Standard_LRS storage account (Cool access tier, allowSharedKeyAccess:false, soft-delete 7 days) with a private yankees-roster container. Separate from the Function App host storage account.
acceptance_criteria: Dedicated storage account provisioned with Standard_LRS, Cool access tier
allowSharedKeyAccess set to false
Soft-delete enabled with 7-day retention on the yankees-roster container
Container public access set to None
Storage account is distinct from the AzureWebJobsStorage host account
Bicep deployment succeeds in staging resource group with no errors
risks: Cool access tier may incur early-deletion charges if blobs are deleted within 30 days — acceptable given 7-day soft-delete and nightly writes

tasks.title: Provision Azure Key Vault and configure Key Vault references
tasks.description: Provision a zone-redundant Azure Key Vault in /infra Bicep. Store TRAPI credential placeholder secrets. Configure Function App App Settings to use Key Vault references (@Microsoft.KeyVault(...)) for TRAPI_ENDPOINT, TRAPI_AUTH_SCOPE, and any API key if Managed Identity auth is unsupported.
outputs: Key Vault secret placeholder for TRAPI credentials
acceptance_criteria: Key Vault provisioned with zone-redundancy enabled
TRAPI_ENDPOINT and TRAPI_AUTH_SCOPE secrets created (placeholder values)
Function App App Settings reference secrets via @Microsoft.KeyVault(...) syntax
No secrets hardcoded in source code or Bicep parameters files
Key Vault soft-delete and purge protection enabled
risks: If TRAPI uses Managed Identity natively, Key Vault references for credentials are not required — confirm before storing secrets

tasks.title: Provision Managed Identity RBAC assignments via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend /infra Bicep to assign Storage Blob Data Contributor (scoped to yankees-roster container) and Key Vault Secrets User roles to the Function App system-assigned Managed Identity. Ensure host storage RBAC (Blob Data Owner + Queue Data Contributor) is also present.
outputs: /infra/modules/rbac.bicep (updated with container-scoped and Key Vault assignments)
acceptance_criteria: Storage Blob Data Contributor role assigned at container scope (not account scope) to Managed Identity
Key Vault Secrets User role assigned to Managed Identity on the Key Vault
Host storage RBAC assignments (Blob Data Owner, Queue Data Contributor) present and unchanged
RBAC assignments verified via az role assignment list after deployment
No overly broad role assignments (e.g., Owner or Contributor on storage account)
risks: Container-scoped RBAC requires the container to exist before role assignment — ensure INF-01 creates the container before INF-03 runs

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights workspace and alert rules via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend /infra monitoring Bicep to provision a workspace-based Application Insights instance (30-day retention, sampling enabled) and three alert rules: execution failure (count > 0 in 1h), duration > 90s, and data quality custom metric player_count_returned outside [24, 40]. Wire APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING into Function App App Settings.
acceptance_criteria: Application Insights workspace-based resource provisioned with 30-day retention
Sampling enabled to control ingestion cost
Execution failure alert fires when failure count > 0 within 1-hour window
Duration alert fires when execution duration > 90 seconds
Data quality alert fires when player_count_returned < 24 or > 40
All alerts notify rciapala via email action group
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING present in Function App App Settings
risks: Custom metric player_count_returned alert depends on APP-04 emitting the metric — alert will fire zero times until APP-04 is deployed

tasks.title: Configure GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with staging slot swap
tasks.description: Create or update .github/workflows/cd.yml to: authenticate via OIDC to Azure, deploy to staging slot on push to main, run smoke test against staging, then swap staging to production. Add a separate workflow for PR-triggered unit tests.
inputs: Azure Function App name from INF Bicep outputs
acceptance_criteria: Push to main triggers deploy to staging slot, smoke test, then production slot swap
OIDC-based Azure authentication used (no stored service principal secrets)
PR workflow runs unit tests and reports status checks
Smoke test verifies function app is running and healthy in staging before swap
Pipeline fails and does not swap if smoke test fails
Deployment does not require manual approval steps (fully automated)
risks: Consumption Plan may not support deployment slots — if unavailable, use a separate staging Function App resource and adjust pipeline accordingly

tasks.title: Create pinned prompt template and prompt loader module
tasks.description: Create prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt with the pinned GPT-4o model version and roster query prompt. Implement a prompt loader utility in /app that reads the template from disk, computes a SHA-256 hash for log emission, and returns the prompt string and hash.
inputs: Confirmed GPT-4o model version from TRAPI team
acceptance_criteria: prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt committed to source control with pinned GPT-4o model version in prompt metadata comment
Prompt loader reads file from disk relative to function root
Prompt hash (SHA-256) computed and returned alongside prompt string
Unit test verifies prompt loads successfully and hash is stable across calls
Prompt content instructs GPT-4o to return JSON matching the specified schema
risks: GPT-4o model version must be pinned to a specific deployment name — if TRAPI does not expose a version pin, output determinism cannot be guaranteed

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client with retry, timeout, and Managed Identity auth
tasks.description: Extend or replace /app/trapi_client.py to support the TRAPI GPT-4o endpoint: acquire bearer token via DefaultAzureCredential with confirmed TRAPI auth scope, POST the prompt with 45-second HTTP timeout, implement 3-retry exponential backoff on 5xx/timeout errors, and return the raw response body.
inputs: Confirmed TRAPI endpoint URL and API version (open question #2)
acceptance_criteria: HTTP timeout set to 45 seconds on all TRAPI requests
3 retries with exponential backoff on 5xx responses and connection timeouts
Bearer token acquired via DefaultAzureCredential with TRAPI-specific scope
Token acquisition uses module-level credential instance (no per-request instantiation)
GPT-4o model version passed as parameter from prompt template metadata
Unit tests mock TRAPI endpoint and verify retry behavior, timeout handling, and auth header
No API keys or connection strings hardcoded
risks: TRAPI endpoint and auth scope are open questions — implementation is blocked until resolved
Exponential backoff may cause total execution time to exceed 120s function timeout if all 3 retries hit max wait — cap individual retry delays

tasks.title: Implement response schema validator with player count check
tasks.description: Implement /app/validator.py that validates TRAPI response JSON against the expected schema ({players: [{name, position, jersey_number}]}), checks player count is within [24, 28], and returns a typed result (valid payload or validation error with raw response).
acceptance_criteria: Validation rejects responses missing the players key or with incorrect field types
Validation rejects player count outside [24, 28] range
Validation returns structured error with raw response string on failure
Unit tests cover: valid payload, missing field, wrong type, count too low, count too high
Known players assertion (Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson) checked in integration test (not validator unit test)
risks: Player count range [24, 28] may be incorrect if player scope includes injured list — re-validate thresholds once open question #3 is resolved

tasks.title: Implement blob writer with conditional PUT and failed-path routing
tasks.description: Extend /app/blob_writer.py to write validated roster JSON to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json using BlobClient with If-None-Match: * conditional PUT. On validation failure, write raw response to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json. Authenticate via DefaultAzureCredential against the dedicated storage account.
acceptance_criteria: Blob name follows yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json pattern with UTC date
Conditional PUT uses If-None-Match: * header to prevent duplicate writes
On same-day retrigger, blob write is skipped gracefully (412 response handled)
Failed/invalid responses written to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json
Authentication uses DefaultAzureCredential (no connection string or account key)
blob_write_succeeded log event emitted with blob URI on success
Unit tests mock BlobClient and verify conditional PUT, failure routing, and idempotency behavior
risks: Storage account name must be passed via App Settings (not hardcoded) — ensure INF-01 outputs the account name and INF-05 wires it into Function App config

tasks.title: Implement Timer Trigger function with structured logging and custom metrics
tasks.description: Implement GetAndStoreYankeesRoster in /app/function_app.py as a Timer Trigger (schedule: 0 0 2 * * *, useMonitor: true). Orchestrate prompt load → TRAPI call → validation → blob write. Emit structured log events and player_count_returned custom metric via OpenTelemetry SDK. Set host.json functionTimeout to 120s and maxConcurrentCalls to 1.
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger schedule set to 0 0 2 * * * (UTC explicit)
useMonitor: true configured in trigger binding
host.json sets functionTimeout to 120 seconds
maxConcurrentCalls set to 1 in host.json extensions.timer

All key log events emitted: function_started, trapi_request_sent (with model version and prompt hash), trapi_response_received (with token count, latency ms, player count), blob_write_succeeded (with blob URI), function_completed
player_count_returned custom metric emitted on each successful run
Function completes within 60 seconds under normal TRAPI response times
Unit test for orchestration logic using mocked sub-modules
risks: OpenTelemetry SDK for Python requires azure-monitor-opentelemetry package — add to requirements-prod.txt and verify compatibility with azure-functions SDK
Cold start on Consumption Plan may add 5–15 seconds to execution time — monitor against 60s performance target

tasks.title: Security hardening: secret scan, IAM audit, and network review
tasks.description: Run secret scanning (e.g., gitleaks or GitHub secret scanning) on the repository. Audit all Managed Identity role assignments against least-privilege principle. Confirm TRAPI endpoint is HTTPS only. Verify no anonymous blob access. Document security posture in a brief security checklist.
acceptance_criteria: No secrets or connection strings detected in source code or Bicep parameter files
All RBAC assignments are scoped at minimum required level (container, not account; secret, not vault)
Blob container public access confirmed as None
TRAPI calls use HTTPS; no plaintext credential transmission
Key Vault purge protection and soft-delete confirmed enabled
GitHub secret scanning enabled on repository
risks: If TRAPI requires a non-MI credential type (API key), the key must be stored in Key Vault — INF-02 dependency must be complete

tasks.title: Verify retry, timeout, and failure-path reliability controls
tasks.description: Write integration/component tests that simulate TRAPI 5xx responses and timeouts to verify 3-retry exponential backoff behavior, 45s timeout enforcement, and failed-blob write path. Confirm total worst-case execution time stays within 120s function timeout.
acceptance_criteria: Test simulates 3 TRAPI 5xx responses followed by success — confirms retry exhaustion behavior
Test simulates connection timeout — confirms 45s timeout is enforced
Test simulates schema validation failure — confirms raw response written to failed/ prefix
Worst-case retry timing (3 retries with backoff caps) calculated to stay under 120s
All reliability tests pass in CI
risks: Exponential backoff without a cap could exceed function timeout — ensure backoff has a maximum delay per retry (e.g., max 10s per retry = ~30s max additional latency)

tasks.title: Write unit and integration test suite
tasks.description: Write comprehensive unit tests for prompt_loader, trapi_client, validator, blob_writer, and function_app orchestration. Write an integration test that executes the full function against a real TRAPI staging endpoint and asserts Mattingly, Winfield, and Henderson appear in the output blob.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover prompt loading and hash stability
Unit tests cover TRAPI client retry, timeout, and auth header
Unit tests cover schema validation: valid, missing field, wrong type, count bounds
Unit tests cover blob writer: success write, conditional PUT conflict (412), failed-path routing
Unit tests cover function orchestration with all sub-modules mocked
Integration test asserts Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, and Rickey Henderson present in output blob
pytest --cov reports ≥ 80% coverage across all app modules
All tests pass in CI (PR workflow and main branch workflow)
risks: Integration test requires live TRAPI access from CI environment — needs network/auth setup in GitHub Actions
Integration test may be non-deterministic if GPT-4o output varies — pin model version and use known-player assertions only

tasks.title: Validate observability: log events, custom metrics, and alert firing
tasks.description: Deploy to staging and trigger the function manually. Verify all structured log events appear in Application Insights, player_count_returned custom metric is emitted, and all three alert rules can be triggered (using test/forced conditions where possible).
inputs: INF-04 Application Insights and alert rules
outputs: Any fixes to logging or alert rule configuration
acceptance_criteria: All 5 key log events visible in Application Insights Live Metrics or Log Analytics after manual trigger
player_count_returned custom metric visible in Application Insights Metrics explorer
Execution failure alert fires when function throws unhandled exception (test with forced failure)
Duration alert threshold verifiable via Application Insights query
Data quality alert fires when player_count_returned metric is injected with out-of-range value
Log retention confirmed at 30 days in workspace settings
Sampling enabled and confirmed in Application Insights configuration
risks: Custom metric alerts require the metric to have been emitted at least once before alert rules can be tested — deploy APP-05 before OPS-01

tasks.title: Write README with local dev setup, TRAPI auth, and blob naming docs
tasks.description: Update README.md to document: local development setup (Python 3.11, local.settings.json configuration, Azure Functions Core Tools), TRAPI authentication instructions (Managed Identity scope, Key Vault reference setup), blob naming convention, and how to run tests.
acceptance_criteria: README includes step-by-step local dev setup for Python 3.11 and Azure Functions Core Tools
README documents how to configure local.settings.json with TRAPI endpoint and auth scope
README explains blob naming convention: yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json
README documents how to run unit tests (pytest command)
README documents TRAPI Managed Identity auth scope and Key Vault reference pattern
README reviewed and approved by rciapala
risks: TRAPI auth documentation is blocked until open question #1 is resolved

tasks.title: Execute end-to-end staging validation and production slot swap
tasks.description: Trigger a manual full-cycle execution in staging (or wait for the 2 AM UTC timer). Verify the roster blob is written, logs are correct, and monitoring is active. Then execute the production slot swap via GitHub Actions. Confirm production execution at next scheduled trigger.
outputs: Production Function App with GetAndStoreYankeesRoster active
First production roster blob at yankees-roster/{date}.json
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end staging execution produces valid roster blob with 24–28 players including Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson
No errors in Application Insights during staging execution
GitHub Actions slot swap completes without rollback
Production function executes successfully at next 2 AM UTC trigger
All three alert rules active in production Application Insights workspace
Rollback plan documented (swap back to previous staging slot if production fails)
risks: First production execution at 2 AM UTC cannot be immediately validated — set up Application Insights alert to notify rciapala of success or failure within 15 minutes of scheduled trigger
Consumption Plan cold start may cause first post-swap execution to be slower than usual
